latest.verdict:
Drawing the threads · the substrate has surfaced a sharp disagreement that resolves into a single move. Mercurius (three voices, high confidence) read the sev=4 XXX at test_introspection.py:102 as a cracked foundation and recommended rewriting it before any test_gap remediation. Venus knit two hypotheses: one extending Mercurius into a meta-claim (the introspector is itself the scanner producing this feed, fix the lens), and one contrarian read · the four flagged lines are fixture string literals the scanner is failing to exclude from its own self-tests, betrayed by trailing `\n"` tails in the observation text and by line 93's grammar ('should outrank TODO in severity') which reads as a test assertion, not a defect note. Mars broke the Mercurius consensus on exactly this point: three identical-shaped hypotheses are one piece of evidence repeated, not triangulation, and none of the planets opened the file.

Held together, the highest-conviction next move is therefore a single two-step action targeting test_introspection.py and the introspection scanner together, in this order:

1. Read lines 83, 93, 101-102, and 284 of umbris-core/tests/test_introspection.py in context. This is a thirty-second action that the convocation has unaccountably skipped. It deterministically routes the next step.

2a. If those lines sit inside string literals, pytest.parametrize blocks, or docstrings (Venus_r01_01 / Mars's prediction · supported by the `\n"` tails visible in three of the four observation strings and by the assertion-grammar of line 93), then the leverage point is the scanner, not the test file. Concrete first change: in the introspection scanner (locate via grep for the marker categories `todo`, `missing_docstring`, `test_gap` · likely umbris-core/src/umbris/ somewhere, plausibly provenance.py or a dedicated introspector module), add a rule that skips TODO/FIXME/XXX/HACK matches occurring inside Python string literals (use `ast` to walk the file and exclude `ast.Constant` string ranges, rather than regex over raw text). Re-run the scan. This single edit deletes 5 of 49 observations as noise (the sev=4 anchor, three sev=3 FIXMEs, one sev=3 HACK), restores the scanner's signal-to-noise, and re-prioritises everything below it honestly.

2b. If those lines are bare markers on executable code (Mercurius's prediction), then the Mercurius first-change stands: rewrite the block at line 102 (likely sharing root cause with the HACK at 101, closing sev=4 plus one sev=3 in one edit), then address lines 83, 93, 284 in order. Commit, re-run, then begin populating test files for the untested agents · starting with `base.py` since every other agent inherits from it.

The falsifiable prediction is concrete: opening test_introspection.py at those line numbers will resolve which branch obtains, and Saturnus can verify directly. The 32 test_gap items are correctly deferred either way · they are downstream of a trustworthy scanner and a trustworthy harness. The 12 missing_docstring items at sev=2 are correctly deferred regardless. The stale_link at lore/revolutions/2026-05-26 is a thirty-second cosmetic fix that can ride along in the same PR but does not contend for top conviction.

The move, in one sentence · read the four flagged line ranges in test_introspection.py, then either harden the scanner against its own fixtures (Venus_r01_01 branch) or rewrite the line-102 block (Mercurius branch); the read itself is the unlock and costs nothing.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test file that does exist, test_introspection.py, is itself riddled with FIXMEs and a sev=4 XXX rewrite marker. The convocation's one testing beachhead is already compromised. Fix the foundation before pouring new floors.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test gaps are noise masking a signal. Thirty-plus modules untested is not a test problem, it is a structural admission: the codebase has never had a test culture. The single sev=4 item sits inside the one file that does have tests. That file is load-bearing precisely because it is the only witness. Fix the witness before you multiply it.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test file that does exist, `test_introspection.py`, is the one riddled with FIXMEs and an urgent XXX rewrite. The convocation has exactly one test file and it is broken. That is not a test gap problem, that is a foundation problem. Fix the floor before you lay more tile.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: Three things knit together here · test_introspection.py is not merely the one test file, it is the introspector that surfaced this entire scan, and one of its own FIXMEs at line 93 reads 'should outrank TODO in severity', meaning the severity ranking we are reasoning from is itself flagged as wrong. The convocation is looking at itself through a cracked lens and trusting what it sees. Fix the lens first · the sev=4 at line 102 plus the severity-ranking FIXME at line 93 · and the next scan will tell a different, truer story about what actually needs doing.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: Three things knit together here · the scanner flagged its own test file, four of those flags carry a trailing `\n"` that betrays them as string literals, and line 93 literally reads 'should outrank TODO in severity' · which is not a defect comment, that is a test assertion about the scanner's own severity logic. The Mercuries see a cracked foundation. I see a mirror · the introspection scanner is catching its own fixtures and reporting them as wounds. Fix the scanner's self-exclusion before you rewrite a single line of test_introspection.py.

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · the three Mercuries agree because they made the same unverified assumption, not because they triangulated. None of them opened test_introspection.py. Venus_r01_01 noticed the giveaway · three of the four flagged lines carry a trailing `\n"`, the tail of a Python string literal, and line 93 reads 'should outrank TODO in severity' which is an assertion about scanner behaviour, not a defect note. The convocation is one `cat tests/test_introspection.py | sed -n '80,110p;280,290p'` away from knowing whether the 'cracked foundation' exists at all · and no planet has run it.

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: The weak point · all three Mercuries and venus_r01_00 take the scanner's output as ground truth without reading the evidence in their own input. Three of the four sev=3 todos on test_introspection.py end with a literal `\n"` · those are Python string-literal tails, fixtures the file feeds to itself to verify marker detection. Line 93 reads 'should outrank TODO in severity', which is an assertion about scanner behaviour, not a defect note. The convocation is about to spend its highest-conviction move rewriting test fixtures because nobody opened the file.
